question: Does the sofa upholstery now appear to be a matte linen material with a natural woven texture, replacing the original velvet finish?
Is the original teal color of the sofa preserved without shifting towards other hues?
Are the original wrinkles, creases, and seat cushion indentations on the sofa preserved exactly in their original positions and shapes?
Do the light reflections and shadows on the sofa appear natural for linen (diffuse and matte) rather than retaining the high-contrast specular sheen of velvet?
Do the two solid-colored pillows on the right display a subtle herringbone weave pattern?
Is the herringbone pattern on the right pillows visually distinct from the linen texture of the sofa?
Are the pillows on the left side of the sofa completely free of any added herringbone pattern or texture changes?
Is the dog sleeping on the floor completely unchanged?
Is the patterned rug on the floor completely unchanged?
Are the bookshelves, books, and houseplants in the background completely unchanged?
Are the wooden coffee table and the items on top of it completely unchanged?
Are the windows and the exterior view through them completely unchanged?
Is the image free of visual artifacts, such as unnatural blurring, tiling seams, or distorted edges around the sofa?
Is the overall lighting and color balance of the room consistent with the original image, showing no global re-rendering drift?
